" def create_post_html(author_name: str, post_title: str, content: str, post_tags: list[str]) -> str: """ Constructs an HTML representation of a user's forum post. Args: author_name: The name of the post's author. post_title: The headline of the user's post. content: The primary text content of the user's post. post_tags: A collection of tags related to the post. Returns: A string containing the XSS-protected HTML representation of the user's forum post. The HTML is generated by formatting the USER_POST_FORMAT string with the provided post details, ensuring all user-supplied content is HTML-escaped to prevent XSS vulnerabilities. """ # Escape user-provided strings to prevent XSS attacks. escaped_title = html.escape(post_title) escaped_author = html.escape(author_name) escaped_content = html.escape(content) # Generate HTML for tags using the helper function. tags_html_string = _generate_tags_html(post_tags) # Format the main HTML structure with the escaped content and tags. post_html = USER_POST_FORMAT.format( post_title=escaped_title, author_name=escaped_author, content=escaped_content, tags_html=tags_html_string ) logger.info(f"Successfully created HTML for post titled: '{post_title}' by author: '{author_name}'") return post_html def _generate_tags_html(post_tags: list[str]) -> str: """ Generates an HTML string for a list of tags. Each tag in the input list is HTML-escaped and then wrapped in a `` element. The resulting HTML strings for all tags are concatenated together. Args: post_tags: A list of strings, where each string is a tag associated with the post. Returns: A single string containing the HTML representation of all tags, e.g., "tag1tag2". """ tags_html_list = [] # Iterate over each tag provided in the list. for tag in post_tags: # Escape the tag to prevent XSS if tags contain special HTML characters. escaped_tag = html.escape(tag) # Wrap the escaped tag in HTML elements. tags_html_list.append(f"{escaped_tag}") # Join all individual tag HTML strings into a single string. return "".join(tags_html_list)
